Migrations

# Switching AI vendors? We do mid-flight handoffs.

Free audit, stabilize before changing anything, incremental migration. The previous vendor's mistake doesn't have to be your ongoing problem.

[Start with the free audit →](/audits)[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)

## Reasons we hear most

- Previous team shipped a demo, then quality decayed in production
- Vendor rates outgrew the value being delivered
- Previous agency disappeared mid-engagement
- Code was opaque — no eval suite, no observability, hard to evolve
- Compliance review found gaps the previous vendor wouldn't address
- Internal team can't extend what the previous vendor built without rewriting

## How a migration goes

1. 1

### Free audit · 5 days

We take read-only access to what's running. 1-page audit back: what's salvageable, what needs replacement, what to fix first. Honest, even if the answer is 'most of this should stay.'
2. 2

### Stabilize before changing

First 2 weeks: we add observability, seed the eval suite from production, document refusal patterns. We don't touch behavior until we can measure regression.
3. 3

### Incremental migration

We migrate one workflow at a time, with eval-gated cutovers. No big-bang rewrite. Each migration is shippable on its own and reversible.
4. 4

### Full ownership transfer

Everything ends up in your accounts, your repos, your observability stack. Same as a greenfield engagement — just with more cleanup upfront.

## FAQ

- ### What if the previous vendor refuses to hand off cleanly?

It happens. We've migrated engagements where the previous vendor wouldn't share prompts or wouldn't provide observability access. We work from what's exposed externally — black-box probing, traffic analysis, log inspection. It costs more time but it works.
- ### Do you slag the previous vendor?

No. We document what's working and what isn't, with evidence. Vendor relationships are messy enough without a successor agency making it personal. We focus forward.
- ### How long does a migration take?

Depends on what's there. A clean handoff from a competent prior vendor: 4-6 weeks. A messy 'why is this even running' situation: 8-12 weeks. We give you a real estimate after the 5-day audit, not before.
- ### What if my contract with the previous vendor isn't expired?

We can plan the migration in parallel and execute when the contract closes. Discovery and audit work happens with your own access, no involvement from them.
- ### What does this cost vs. a fresh build?

Audit phase is free. Stabilization adds ~2 weeks vs. a greenfield project. Migration itself is usually within 20-30% of a clean Fixed-fee Sprint. The math is usually compelling because you're not starting from zero.

## Tell us what you're inheriting

A paragraph about what's running, who built it, what's broken. We'll come back within a business day with whether an audit makes sense — and what it'd cover.

Office hours · free

# 30 minutes of honest scoping.

Every other Thursday, we run free 30-minute office hours. Bring an AI problem — early-stage, fuzzy, half-formed. Leave with a decision and an honest read.

Different from a discovery call — office hours are for problems you're not ready to buy yet. Discovery is for problems you're close to scoping.

## What happens in 30 minutes

- ### You describe your problem

5 minutes. The fuzzier the better — office hours are designed for the early-stage questions, not the late-stage scoping.
- ### We ask the hard questions

20 minutes. Who's the owner? What's the data look like? What's the deflection math? Has it been tried before?
- ### You leave with a decision

5 minutes. Build now, build later, don't build at all. We'll be honest about which one. Email follow-up with the punch list.

## Ground rules

- Real problems only — not 'I want to learn about AI in general'
- One topic per session — bring your highest-leverage question
- Bring a stakeholder if the decision-maker isn't you
- Recordings off by default · we don't keep the call data after the email follow-up
- Not a sales call · we'll tell you if we're not the right team

## Request a slot

Tell us what you'd bring. We'll come back within a business day with a Thursday slot that fits — or honestly tell you office hours isn't the right format.

Honest comparisons

# Should you work with us — or with one of these?

Three honest comparisons. We tell you when the other option is the right one. The pitch only works because the comparisons are real.

- [vs In-house team

## Alian AI vs hiring in-house

When it makes sense to hire your own AI engineers — and when it costs more than it should.

Read the comparison](/vs/in-house)
- [vs US/EU agency

## Alian AI vs a US/EU AI agency

Same caliber of engineering. Very different invoice. The math is the math.

Read the comparison](/vs/agency)
- [vs Big 4 consultancy

## Alian AI vs Big 4 / Tier-1 consultancy

Different products. Strategy decks vs working code. Pick the one that actually ships.

Read the comparison](/vs/consultancy)

## Quick takes

vs Hiring an in-house AI team
:   Hiring in-house is the right answer for some companies.
[Full comparison →](/vs/in-house)

vs Working with a US/EU AI agency
:   When you've shortlisted a US or EU AI agency, you're paying $150–$250/hour for senior engineers.
[Full comparison →](/vs/agency)

vs Big 4 / Tier-1 AI consultancy
:   A Big 4 AI engagement is a strategy product — slides, frameworks, governance, change management.
[Full comparison →](/vs/consultancy)

## Not sure which fits?

Book a 20-min call. We'll tell you honestly which option is right for your situation — even if it's not us.

[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)[See pricing](/pricing)

3. AI Agents & Automation

Service pillar

# AI Agents & Automation

When your business needs hands that don't sleep.

Lead-qualification agents, support agents, multi-step ops agents, and workflow automation. Production-grade systems that watch, decide, and act — with humans in the loop where it matters.

[Book a 20-min scoping call →](/book-a-demo)[Try our AI on your problem ↑](/#hero-input)

## What you get

- Custom AI agents scoped to a specific business outcome — not generic chatbots
- n8n / LangGraph / Anthropic Agents SDK orchestration for multi-step workflows
- Voice and telephony agents via Vapi, Retell, or Twilio when conversation needs to happen on the phone
- Full observability — every agent decision logged with reasoning, replayable, evaluatable
- Human-in-loop checkpoints baked in by default. Your team owns the escalation logic.

## When this is the right fit

- You have a repetitive ops workflow that's eating senior time
- Inbound volume is outrunning your team's bandwidth
- You want to automate but not at the cost of brand or accuracy

## Sample builds

- ### Inbound lead qualification agent

Multi-channel (web, email, SMS, voice) qualification that engages every lead in under 60 seconds and books AEs only when they should be talking.
- ### Customer-support resolution agent

Triage, reproduce, resolve — for everything from password resets to autonomous code-fix PRs through Claude Code.
- ### 24/7 ops monitor

Multi-agent system mirroring an org chart — production, maintenance, quality, inventory — coordinating through a shared event bus.

## Tech we reach for

- Anthropic Claude
- LangGraph
- Anthropic Agents SDK
- n8n (self-hosted)
- Vapi / Retell / Twilio
- LangSmith / Langfuse
- Postgres / Redis

## Related case studies

- [Manufacturing

### Multi-Agent System for 24/7 Manufacturing Plant

31% · Reduction in unplanned downtime · 90 days](/work/multi-agent-manufacturing)
- [B2B SaaS / DevTools

### Auto Issue Resolution: Chatbot + Claude Code

12 min · Average ticket → merged PR time (down from ~6 days)](/work/auto-issue-resolution)
- [B2B SaaS

### Inbound Lead Qualification Agent

47% · Increase in booked demos (64 → 94/month)](/work/lead-qualification-agent)

## FAQ

- ### How is this different from a generic chatbot?

A chatbot answers questions. An agent runs a workflow — fetches data, calls tools, decides between branches, escalates to humans when its confidence is low. We build the second one.
- ### Can the agent touch our production systems?

Yes, with guardrails. We use scoped credentials, allowlisted actions, and explicit human approval for anything irreversible. See the Auto Issue Resolution case study for an end-to-end example.

## Talk to a human about this.

20 min. No deck. We'll tell you what's possible — and what isn't.

[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)[See pricing](/pricing)

When your users need answers they can ask for.](/services/apps-chatbots)

When you need a plan before a build.](/services/strategy)

When your data is begging to be useful.](/services/data-intelligence)

[EN](/en/audits)[HI](/hi/audits)

Free AI audit

# Shipped AI? We'll audit it for free.

If you've already deployed an AI feature, send us read-only access. We'll come back in 5 business days with a 1-page audit: what's working, what's at risk, what we'd fix in week one.

Different from [office hours](/office-hours) — audits are deeper and depend on real access to your system.

## What we look at

- ### Eval coverage

Is there an eval suite? How big? When was it last run? What did it catch — or miss?
- ### Refusal + escalation patterns

What does the agent refuse to do? What's the escalation path? Where does it fail gracefully vs hallucinate confidently?
- ### Observability + audit trail

Can you reproduce a conversation that went wrong last Tuesday? Are reasoning chains logged? Who owns the dashboards?
- ### Cost + quality telemetry

Do you know the cost per conversation, per user, per workflow? Is there a quality score trend over time?

### Who this is for

- You've shipped an AI feature that's been live 30+ days
- Customer feedback is mixed — some 'magic,' some 'AI mistakes'
- Engineering says it's fine. Compliance is skeptical. Leadership wants a third opinion.
- You're thinking about scaling the AI but want sanity-check first

### Not for

- You haven't shipped anything yet — talk to us about a Strategy phase
- You want a marketing-quality vendor evaluation. Audits are engineering-level.
- You can't share read-only access to prompts, eval suite (if any), and observability dashboards
- You're hoping to use the audit as ammunition in an internal political fight

## What you get

- One-page written summary: what's working, what's at risk, what we'd fix in week 1
- 30-min walkthrough call to discuss the findings
- Optional: a 90-min deep-dive workshop with your team (paid, separate)

Audits are free because they're how we vet whether we'd want to work with you next. We do 2 per month — first-come, first-served.

## Request an audit

Tell us what you've shipped. We'll come back within a business day with a yes / no / not-now, plus the access checklist if it's a yes.

Name

Work email

Company

What kind of conversation?20-min discovery — scope a possible build60-min technical deep-dive — architecture reviewAgency / partner inquiryOther

Send

We reply within one business day. Usually faster.

Roadmap

# What we're shipping on this site.

Now / Next / Later / Shelved. Updated when reality changes. The [changelog](/changelog) is the past tense of this page.

## Now

Actively shipping

- ### Real founder bios + headshots

On /about and /careers. Waiting on the photoshoot — content is drafted, photos drop next.
- ### Real client logos

Replacing the placeholders on the homepage trust strip. Waiting on sign-off from 4 clients.
- ### Calendly embed on /book-a-demo

Already wired — flips on the moment NEXT\_PUBLIC\_CALENDLY\_URL is set. Picking the slot duration mix.

## Next

Queued for the next 30-60 days

- ### External uptime monitor

Hooking /status into a real uptime probe service. Historical graphs, SLA badges, incident timeline.
- ### Customer success story long-form

Separate from case studies — narrative-first, video transcripts, lessons learned.
- ### i18n scaffolding

Locale-aware routing for en/hi to start. Most copy stays English; we'll translate the high-traffic surfaces first.
- ### Live counter band data

Wire the homepage counters to real numbers from problems\_typed and engagements. Static placeholders today.

## Later

On the list, no committed date

- ### Internal admin dashboard

Browse leads, AI conversations, eval-suite results from one place. Behind auth.
- ### Embeddable widget for partner sites

Our Problem Solver as a script tag for partner blogs.
- ### Public model evals leaderboard

Anonymized eval scores across model versions on the use cases we run.
- ### RFC / public design docs

Open patterns we propose to the broader AI-agency community. Comment-friendly.

## Shelved

Considered and parked

- ### Cookie consent banner

Shipped as opt-in only — we have no trackers that require consent. Re-evaluate if we add anything.
- ### Local-first WebGPU agent demos

Shelved until local model quality improves. See /labs for the why.
- ### Pricing toggle USD/EUR/GBP/INR

Considered. Decided against — rates are USD-anchored, conversion happens at invoice time.

## Want something added?

Email [sales@aliansoftware.net](mailto:sales@aliansoftware.net?subject=Roadmap suggestion). We'll respond — and either add it or tell you honestly why we won't.

[See what's shipped →](/changelog)

Learn

# Structured paths through everything we've published.

Pick the path that matches why you're here. We'll walk you through our case studies, playbooks, and tools in the order that makes sense.

For: Founders

## I want to ship AI in my company — where do I start?

A guided path from 'what's possible' to 'what to actually build first' to a 20-min conversation with a human.

~41 min

1. [1Tour the homepage demo3 min](/)
2. [2Take the AI Readiness Quiz5 min](/quiz)
3. [3Read the AI implementation roadmap8 min](/blog/ai-implementation-roadmap-90-days)
4. [4Estimate ROI for your top use case5 min](/roi)
5. [5Book a 20-min discovery call20 min](/book-a-demo)

For: Engineering leaders

## How do I evaluate an AI services partner?

How we engineer AI, what we ship, the patterns we use, and how we handle the hard parts.

~43 min

1. [1Read the engineering methodology7 min](/methodology)
2. [2Read the Production agent design playbook8 min](/playbooks/agent-design)
3. [3Read the Eval suite playbook6 min](/playbooks/eval-suite)
4. [4Read the RAG playbook7 min](/playbooks/rag)
5. [5Skim a case study to see it shipped10 min](/work/auto-issue-resolution)
6. [6Read the API docs for our public endpoints5 min](/api-docs)

For: Compliance + procurement

## Is Alian AI safe to work with?

Security posture, data handling, audit-traceability, vendor evaluation. The packet your reviewers want.

~38 min

1. [1Read the Security overview8 min](/security)
2. [2Read the Privacy Policy6 min](/legal/privacy)
3. [3Read the Responsible disclosure policy5 min](/security/disclosure)
4. [4Read the AI compliance for fintech blog post10 min](/blog/ai-compliance-fintech)
5. [5See the Cookies + accessibility statements4 min](/legal/cookies)
6. [6Email security@aliansoftware.net for the full packet5 min](mailto:security@aliansoftware.net)

For: AI-curious operators

## What can AI actually do for my team?

Practical, non-technical paths through what's possible across your role.

~24 min

1. [1Browse the use cases A–Z5 min](/use-cases)
2. [2Find your industry pattern3 min](/industries)
3. [3Read the e-commerce playbook (or your vertical)8 min](/blog/ecommerce-ai-playbook-2026)
4. [4Take the AI Readiness Quiz5 min](/quiz)
5. [5Try the homepage Live Problem Solver3 min](/#hero-input)

For: Anyone with 30 min and curiosity

## The greatest hits

If you're skimming the site for the first time, these are the pages we'd point you to.

~30 min

1. [1Homepage — see the Live Problem Solver5 min](/)
2. [2Multi-Agent Manufacturing case study8 min](/work/multi-agent-manufacturing)
3. [3Pricing — three engagement models5 min](/pricing)
4. [4The Methodology page7 min](/methodology)
5. [5The Blog5 min](/blog)

## Done reading?

Book a 20-min call with a human. Or describe a problem in the hero and watch the agent scope it.

[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)[Try the AI ↑](/#hero-input)

PDF library

# Downloadable PDFs.

Every PDF on the site, in one place. Case-study leave-behinds for sharing internally, the AI Readiness Checklist, and the strategy artifacts we use on day one of every engagement.

- [AI Readiness Checklist

30 honest questions across data, use cases, governance, and team enablement.

alian-ai-readiness-checklist.pdf · 1 page](/api/checklist)
- [Multi-Agent System for 24/7 Manufacturing Plant — case study

31% · Reduction in unplanned downtime · 90 days

alian-ai-multi-agent-manufacturing.pdf · 1 page](/api/case-study/multi-agent-manufacturing)
- [Auto Issue Resolution: Chatbot + Claude Code — case study

12 min · Average ticket → merged PR time (down from ~6 days)

alian-ai-auto-issue-resolution.pdf · 1 page](/api/case-study/auto-issue-resolution)
- [Alian Infinity AI ERP — case study

4× · Leadership-tier ERP usage increase

alian-ai-alian-infinity-erp.pdf · 1 page](/api/case-study/alian-infinity-erp)
- [Inbound Lead Qualification Agent — case study

47% · Increase in booked demos (64 → 94/month)

alian-ai-lead-qualification-agent.pdf · 1 page](/api/case-study/lead-qualification-agent)
- [YouTube Channel Automation Pipeline — case study

3× · Output growth · 62% lower cost per video

alian-ai-youtube-automation.pdf · 1 page](/api/case-study/youtube-automation)

Testimonials

# What people who paid us actually said.

Real quotes from real engagements. Names withheld where the client asked us to anonymize. Linked to the case study where applicable.

- > "I expected a fancy dashboard. What we got was a team of agents that quietly do the watching so my humans can focus on doing."

Plant Operations Manager

Manufacturing client

[Read the case study →](/work/multi-agent-manufacturing)
- > "The first time I saw a Claude-Code-authored PR ship to production with no human edits, I was nervous. 90 days later I think about it the same way I think about any other contributor."

VP of Engineering

B2B SaaS · DevTools

[Read the case study →](/work/auto-issue-resolution)
- > "The agent qualifies better than my junior SDRs because it never has a bad day. And when it doesn't know, it knows it doesn't know — and routes to a human."

VP of Sales

B2B SaaS

[Read the case study →](/work/lead-qualification-agent)
- > "Our finance team stopped scheduling reports. They just ask. The cost-drift alerts have caught two material variances before close — that alone paid for the AI layer."

Plant Operations Director

Manufacturing & ERP

[Read the case study →](/work/alian-infinity-erp)
- > "We were skeptical that AI could do this without sounding like AI. The trick wasn't the AI — it was Alian's pipeline design. The voice still sounds like me."

Channel host & founder

Creator economy

[Read the case study →](/work/youtube-automation)
- > "Senior engineers from the first commit. Working code at every weekly demo. Fixed-fee that actually meant fixed-fee."

CTO

B2B SaaS
- > "We brought them in for a 6-week sprint and renewed onto a retainer the day it shipped."

Head of Product

E-commerce / D2C

Want to join the renewals club? [Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)

[EN](/en/process)[HI](/hi/process)

How we work

# From "we have a problem" to live in production.

Most engagements ship in 4–8 weeks. Same four steps every time. Fixed price, no surprise invoices, all IP yours from day one.

1. Step 1 · Day 0

20 min

## Discovery call

We listen. You describe the problem in your words. We ask the questions that matter — current workflow, who owns it, what's been tried, what would shipping mean. Free, no deck, no pitch. If we're not the right team, we'll say so on the call.

You walk away with

- Honest go / no-go signal
- Recommended engagement model (sprint / retainer / hourly)
- Rough timeline and budget envelope
2. Step 2 · Day 1–7

1–2 weeks

## Scope & SOW

Deep discovery — interviews, code/data review, technical risk audit. We come back with a fixed-scope SOW: what's in, what's out, what it costs, what could change it. You sign or you don't. No mystery, no surprise invoices, no scope creep.

You walk away with

- Written SOW with milestones and acceptance criteria
- Fixed price (or retainer/hourly structure)
- List of dependencies on your side (access, data, sign-off owners)
3. Step 3 · Week 1–6

4–6 weeks typical

## Build & weekly demos

Senior engineers, working in your repo from week one. Weekly demos — you see real code running, not slides. Bi-weekly retros. Tight feedback loop. We catch direction-changes early when they're cheap.

You walk away with

- Working code at every weekly demo
- Eval suite, observability, and docs as we go
- Production-ready by the final week
4. Step 4 · Week 6+

1–2 weeks

## Ship & hand off

Production launch, runbook handover, knowledge transfer to your team. Code, prompts, agent configs, and ops docs all transferred to your repository. We're available on hourly retainer if you want us for tuning or extensions, but you don't owe us anything beyond the SOW.

You walk away with

- Production deploy
- Runbook + on-call playbook
- All IP transferred to your accounts

## Principles we hold to

- ### Working code beats slides.

We demo working code at every milestone. If we don't have something to show, that's a status problem we resolve before the meeting — not a slide we hide it behind.
- ### Discovery is non-optional.

Most fixed-fee overruns come from skipped discovery. We invest 1–2 weeks upfront specifically to avoid that. If we're not confident, we'll say so and propose a paid discovery sprint first.
- ### Senior on every project.

No juniors learning on your dime. Backed by Alian Software's engineering bench so we don't have to compromise on seniority to meet a price.
- ### Your IP, day one.

Code, prompts, configs — all transferred to your repos from the first commit. No vendor lock-in disguised as 'our framework.'

## Start at Day 0.

20 min. No deck. Pick a slot — or describe your problem first and we'll do the prep.

[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)[Try our AI on it ↑](/#hero-input)

Playbooks

# The patterns we ship — written down.

What we'd brief a new engineer on before they touch a real engagement. Read here or download the PDFs to circulate inside your team.

- [Playbook·8 min·6 pages

## Production agent design

How to design agents that ship — guardrails, escalation, observability.

Read playbook](/playbooks/agent-design)
- [Playbook·7 min·5 pages

## RAG that actually grounds

Retrieval, reranking, citations, refusal. The pattern that stops hallucination.

Read playbook](/playbooks/rag)
- [Playbook·6 min·4 pages

## The eval suite you actually keep running

20 test cases, replayed weekly, evolving with production. The minimum for shipping.

Read playbook](/playbooks/eval-suite)

**We don't track you.** No third-party analytics, no advertising cookies, no fingerprinting. We use one functional cookie for your theme preference. [See the cookies policy →](/legal/cookies)

Legal

# Cookies Policy

Last updated: 14 May 2026

[Privacy](/legal/privacy)[Terms](/legal/terms)[Cookies](/legal/cookies)

Short version: we don't use tracking cookies, third-party advertising cookies, or fingerprinting. We use one functional cookie for the dark-mode preference. That's it.

## What we set

| Name | Purpose | Duration |
| --- | --- | --- |
| theme | Remembers your dark / light mode preference. | 1 year, your device |
| headline\_typed | Session-only flag so the hero headline animation only plays once per visit. Not a cookie — it's `sessionStorage`. | Until tab closes |

## Analytics

We use [Plausible](https://plausible.io) for analytics — but only if you see *An Alian Software company* in the footer with analytics live. Plausible is GDPR-compliant and doesn't use cookies or fingerprinting.

If we ever add a tool that uses cookies, you'll see a consent banner before it loads.

## Server-side storage

When you submit a form, we store the submission in our database to reply to you. That's not a cookie either, but it's worth knowing about. See the [Privacy Policy](/legal/privacy) for what we keep and for how long.

## Opting out

You can clear the `theme` cookie any time from your browser's developer tools or by clearing site data. The site will fall back to your system theme.

Questions about this document? [legal@aliansoftware.net](mailto:legal@aliansoftware.net) — we'll route to the right human.

we'll tell you which model is wrong for you and why.\"]}]\n"])self.\_\_next\_f.push([1,"e:[\"$\",\"section\",null,{\"className\":\"bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] px-4 py-20 sm:px-6 lg:px-8\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mx-auto max-w-3xl\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"text-center text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-[color:var(--color-text)] sm:text-3xl\",\"children\":\"Pricing FAQ\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-center text-sm text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"The objections we hear most — answered upfront.\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-10\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L16\",null,{\"items\":[{\"q\":\"Why are your rates lower than US or European agencies?\",\"a\":\"Geography. We're engineered in India and ship globally. The same senior engineer who'd cost $180/hour at a San Francisco agency lives in Pune or Bangalore and costs us a fraction of that — so we charge a fraction of that, and pass the math to you.\\n\\nWhat we don't discount is what matters: senior engineers (no juniors on your project), production-grade code, clean handoff documentation, and the engineering DNA of Alian Software's 15+ years shipping enterprise systems. Our case studies are how we prove this — read them and decide for yourself whether the work looks like $25/hour work or $250/hour work.\\n\\nIf you've been quoted $150–$250/hour by a US firm and the deliverable feels expensive for what you're getting, our pricing is going to feel uncomfortable until you see the output. After that it just feels obvious.\"},{\"q\":\"Can I start hourly and convert to a fixed-fee sprint later?\",\"a\":\"Yes — and we recommend it for most projects.\\n\\nHourly is the right starting point when the scope is fuzzy. Spend 20–40 hours with us on discovery, prototyping, and a proof-of-concept. By the end of that, we'll both know exactly what the full build looks like — and at that point, we'll convert into a fixed-fee sprint with a clear scope, price, and timeline. No mystery, no surprise invoices.\\n\\nThe conversion is one paragraph in your SOW. Hours already spent count toward the sprint, so you're not paying twice.\"},{\"q\":\"What does the 20-hour-per-month minimum mean?\",\"a\":\"Hourly engagements have a 20-hour minimum per month. Below that, the math stops working — kickoff, context-switching, status updates, and invoicing eat the engagement before any real work happens.\\n\\nIf you genuinely need less than that — say, a 4-hour audit or a one-off code review — book it as a fixed one-time engagement instead. We have a flat fee for those.\"},{\"q\":\"What if the project takes longer than the fixed-fee sprint estimate?\",\"a\":\"That's our problem, not yours. The point of a fixed-fee sprint is that you know the price before we start, and the price doesn't move because we underestimated.\\n\\nTwo important nuances:\\n\\nScope changes — if you change what we're building mid-sprint (\\\"can you also add X?\\\"), we'll quote a scope-change adjustment honestly. Either way, you approve before any work happens.\\n\\nDiscovery quality — most fixed-fee overruns come from bad discovery. We invest 1–2 weeks of upfront discovery before quoting anything, specifically to avoid this. If we don't feel confident in the scope, we'll tell you.\"},{\"q\":\"Who owns the code and IP we build together?\",\"a\":\"You do. 100%. Code, prompts, fine-tuned models, agent definitions, n8n workflows, deployment configs — all transferred to your repository on day one and reassigned to you on project close.\\n\\nWe retain the right to reference the work in our portfolio (with your sign-off on what's public vs. anonymized), nothing more. We don't keep your code on our servers. We don't license you \\\"our framework.\\\" When the engagement ends, your team owns and operates everything we built.\"},{\"q\":\"What's the payment schedule?\",\"a\":\"Fixed-fee Sprint: 30% on signing, 30% at midpoint demo, 40% on delivery. Net-15 invoicing, payable in USD, EUR, GBP, or INR.\\n\\nMonthly Retainer: Invoiced on the 1st of each month, payable by the 15th. Three-month minimum, then month-to-month with 30 days' notice.\\n\\nHourly: Invoiced weekly for the prior week's hours, payable Net-15. Tracked time tool (Toggl) with a detailed weekly hours breakdown.\\n\\nFor first-time clients, we usually request 30% upfront on the first engagement. After that, standard terms.\"},{\"q\":\"What's NOT included in your quotes?\",\"a\":\"Honest answer: things that make engagements run over for everyone. We call them out upfront.\\n\\n· Third-party API costs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Pinecone, Twilio, ElevenLabs) — billed directly to your accounts. No markup.\\n· Infrastructure costs (Vercel, Railway, AWS, GPU compute) — your accounts. No markup.\\n· Custom design work beyond functional UI — we'll bring in a partner designer at their rate.\\n· Compliance certifications — we'll build to SOC 2 / ISO 27001 readiness; the audit is between you and your auditor.\\n· Maintenance and on-call beyond what's specified in the SOW — most clients move to a Monthly Retainer post-launch.\"},{\"q\":\"Do you require a long-term commitment?\",\"a\":\"No.\\n\\nFixed-fee Sprints end when the deliverable ships. No commitment beyond.\\nMonthly Retainer has a 3-month minimum (because that's how long it takes to build the relationship and produce real output), then month-to-month with 30 days' notice.\\nHourly has zero commitment beyond the 20-hour monthly minimum.\\n\\nWe earn renewals with output. If we're not earning yours, we'd rather you leave cleanly than feel locked in.\"},{\"q\":\"Can you work with our existing engineering team?\",\"a\":\"Yes — and most of our retainer clients have us doing exactly this. We embed alongside your team, work in your repos, attend your standups, and follow your code review process. We're additive, not replacement.\\n\\nFor some engagements (especially production-critical agent work), this is the only sensible model — your team needs to own and extend what we build, and the fastest way to ensure that is for us to build it together.\"},{\"q\":\"What about NDAs, MSAs, and DPAs?\",\"a\":\"Standard for us.\\n\\n· NDA — happy to sign yours, or we have a clean mutual NDA template\\n· MSA — we have a template that's been through enough legal reviews to be reasonable; happy to redline yours instead\\n· DPA — required for EU and most enterprise engagements; current GDPR + DPDP-aligned template\\n· SCCs for cross-border data transfers — included in our DPA\\n\\nIf your legal team needs to drive the paper, we'll move at their pace. Pricing

# Engineering excellence at globally competitive rates.

Starting at **$25/hour** for engineering work, **$35/hour** for senior architects and AI specialists. Fixed-fee and retainer engagements quoted on discovery.

- ## Fixed-fee Sprint

Defined deliverable: 'Build us a customer support agent.'

Quoted per project

Scoped on discovery call

Fixed scope, fixed price, fixed timeline. Discovery → SOW → ship.

Timeline: 2–8 weeks

Not for

- · Exploratory work without a defined deliverable
- · Engagements under $20K — overhead eats it

[Discuss this →](/book-a-demo)
- Most popular

## Monthly Retainer

Ongoing AI partner: roadmap, iteration, multiple workstreams in parallel.

Custom

Based on team size and hours/month

Capped hours/month · dedicated team · monthly check-ins · 3-month minimum.

Timeline: 3-month minimum

Not for

- · Single-shot builds with a fixed scope (use Sprint)
- · Teams who can't commit to 3 months

[Discuss this →](/book-a-demo)
- ## Hourly / T&M

Exploratory work, audits, advisory, niche extensions, post-launch tweaks.

$25 / hour

Senior architects & AI specialists $35/hour

Hourly rate · weekly invoicing · easy to start, easy to stop · 20-hour monthly minimum.

Timeline: No commitment

Not for

- · Anything where you need budget certainty upfront
- · Builds with 3+ engineers in parallel

[Discuss this →](/book-a-demo)

[See all three side-by-side →](/pricing/compare)

Not sure which fits? [Book a 20-min call](/book-a-demo) — we'll tell you which model is wrong for you and why.

## Pricing FAQ

The objections we hear most — answered upfront.

- Why are your rates lower than US or European agencies?

Geography. We're engineered in India and ship globally. The same senior engineer who'd cost $180/hour at a San Francisco agency lives in Pune or Bangalore and costs us a fraction of that — so we charge a fraction of that, and pass the math to you.
What we don't discount is what matters: senior engineers (no juniors on your project), production-grade code, clean handoff documentation, and the engineering DNA of Alian Software's 15+ years shipping enterprise systems. Our case studies are how we prove this — read them and decide for yourself whether the work looks like $25/hour work or $250/hour work.
If you've been quoted $150–$250/hour by a US firm and the deliverable feels expensive for what you're getting, our pricing is going to feel uncomfortable until you see the output. After that it just feels obvious.
- Can I start hourly and convert to a fixed-fee sprint later?
- What does the 20-hour-per-month minimum mean?
- What if the project takes longer than the fixed-fee sprint estimate?
- Who owns the code and IP we build together?
- What's the payment schedule?
- What's NOT included in your quotes?
- Do you require a long-term commitment?
- Can you work with our existing engineering team?
- What about NDAs, MSAs, and DPAs?

## Still have questions?

Two ways to get answers — pick whichever feels lighter.

[Ask our AI agent ↑](/#hero-input)[Book a 20-min discovery call →](/book-a-demo)

[EN](/en/checklist)[HI](/hi/checklist)

Free · 30-question checklist

# The AI Readiness Checklist for SMBs.

30 honest questions across data, use cases, governance, and team enablement. Score yourself in 10 minutes. Get a 1-page summary of what to fix before you build, what to build first, and what to defer.

- ## Data readiness

8 questions

What's accessible, what's clean, what's siloed, and where the AI you want to ship will hit walls.
- ## Use-case clarity

7 questions

How specific your use cases are, whether the ROI math is honest, and whether you've prioritized correctly.
- ## Governance & risk

8 questions

Privacy, security, compliance, model selection, and the policies that have to exist before procurement signs.
- ## Team enablement

7 questions

Who needs to build, who needs to ship, who needs to govern — and what training closes the gaps.

Name

Work email

Company

Send me the checklist

No newsletter spam. We email it once, then leave you alone.

The PDF is the same checklist we use on day one of every Strategy engagement.

# अपनी समस्या बताएं। हम उसे हल करने के लिए AI आर्किटेक्ट करेंगे।

हर व्यवसाय में एक workflow है जो AI की मांग कर रहा है। नीचे अपना workflow लिखें — हमारा agent सेकंडों में एक solution तैयार करेगा। फिर इसे बनाने के लिए किसी इंसान से बात करें।

हमारी सपोर्ट टीम दोहराए जाने वाले टिकट्स में दबी हुई है

इसे हल करें

हमारा AI लगभग 8 सेकंड में समाधान का स्कोप तैयार करता है। साइनअप की आवश्यकता नहीं। सबमिट करने के लिए ⌘/Ctrl + Enter दबाएं।

Engineered by the team behind Alian Software · 15+ years shipping enterprise systems

Live agent activity

- YOUTUBEपाइपलाइन ने एपिसोड 142 के लिए 6 शॉर्ट-फॉर्म वेरिएंट जनरेट किए0s ago
- LEADलीड क्वालिफिकेशन agent → AE को इन्क्वायरी रूट की23s ago
- SUPPORTऑटो-रिज़ॉल्यूशन: टिकट #4729 फिक्स हुआ और PR मर्ज हुआ17s ago

How we work

## From "we have a problem" to live in production. The whole arc.

Most engagements ship in 4–6 weeks. Here's the timeline.

- Day 0

### Discovery call

20 min · We listen, we ask, we scope.
- Day 7

### Scope & SOW

Fixed price · No surprises.
- Week 4–6

### Build & weekly demos

Senior engineering team · Working code from day one.
- Week 6+

### Live & iterating

Your team owns it · We support if you want us to.

[Start at Day 0 →](/book-a-demo)

What we build

## Whatever the problem looks like, one of these is the solution.

- [### AI Agents & Automation

When your business needs hands that don't sleep.

Lead-qualification agents, support agents, ops automation. n8n, LangGraph, voice agents.

Learn more →](/services/agents-automation)
- [### Custom AI Apps & Chatbots

When your users need answers they can ask for.

RAG-powered chatbots, knowledge bases, custom LLM apps. Production-grade, branded, multitenant.

›

Learn more →](/services/apps-chatbots)
- [### AI Strategy & Enablement

When you need a plan before a build.

AI roadmap, governance, team training. For leaders figuring out where AI fits in the next 12 months.

Data readiness—

Use-case clarity—

Team enablement—

Learn more →](/services/strategy)
- [### Data & Intelligence

When your data is begging to be useful.

Predictive ML, computer vision, generative content pipelines. Turn data into decisions.

Learn more →](/services/data-intelligence)

Real builds

## Real problems. Real builds. Real numbers.

- Production
- Maintenance
- Quality
- Inventory
- Energy
- Briefing

ManufacturingAgents & Automation

### Multi-Agent System for 24/7 Manufacturing Plant

31%

Reduction in unplanned downtime · 90 days

Six specialized agents watching production, maintenance, quality, inventory, energy, and shift handoffs — coordinating through a shared event bus, alerting humans via WhatsApp.

- 8×

faster anomaly detection
- 0

stockouts (vs 7 prior)
- 9%

energy savings

[Read the full case study →](/work/multi-agent-manufacturing)

Why teams choose us

## Engineering rigor, agency speed, global pricing.

- ### Production-ready in 6 weeks.

We ship working code from week one — not slides, not theory.
- ### Backed by Alian Software's engineering bench.

15+ years of enterprise software DNA. No juniors on your project.
- ### Your data, your models, your IP.

Code transferred on day one. Prompts, agents, configs — all yours to own and extend.
- ### Globally competitive rates · starting at $25/hr.

Engineered in India, shipping globally. Same senior talent at a fraction of the SF rate.

What clients say

## Three quotes from the people writing the renewal checks.

- > "मैंने एक शानदार डैशबोर्ड की उम्मीद की थी। हमें जो मिला वह agents की एक टीम थी जो चुपचाप निगरानी का काम करती है ताकि मेरे लोग काम पर ध्यान केंद्रित कर सकें।"

प्लांट ऑपरेशन्स मैनेजर

विनिर्माण क्लाइंट

मल्टी-एजेंट ऑपरेशन सिस्टम · 31% डाउनटाइम में कमी
- > "पहली बार जब मैंने Claude-Code द्वारा लिखा गया PR बिना किसी मानवीय संपादन के production में जाते देखा, तो मैं घबरा गया था। 90 दिन बाद अब मैं इसके बारे में उसी तरह सोचता हूँ जैसे किसी अन्य contributor के बारे में सोचता हूँ।"

इंजीनियरिंग के वाइस प्रेजिडेंट

B2B SaaS · DevTools

स्वचालित-समाधान चैटबॉट + Claude Code
- > "एजेंट मेरे जूनियर SDRs से बेहतर क्वालिफाई करता है क्योंकि इसका कभी बुरा दिन नहीं होता। और जब यह नहीं जानता, तो यह जानता है कि यह नहीं जानता — और किसी इंसान को रूट कर देता है।"

Legal

# Cookies Policy

Last updated: 14 May 2026

[Privacy](/legal/privacy)[Terms](/legal/terms)[Cookies](/legal/cookies)

Short version: we don't use tracking cookies, third-party advertising cookies, or fingerprinting. We use one functional cookie for the dark-mode preference. That's it.

## What we set

| Name | Purpose | Duration |
| --- | --- | --- |
| theme | Remembers your dark / light mode preference. | 1 year, your device |
| headline\_typed | Session-only flag so the hero headline animation only plays once per visit. Not a cookie — it's `sessionStorage`. | Until tab closes |

## Analytics

We use [Plausible](https://plausible.io) for analytics — but only if you see *An Alian Software company* in the footer with analytics live. Plausible is GDPR-compliant and doesn't use cookies or fingerprinting.

If we ever add a tool that uses cookies, you'll see a consent banner before it loads.

## Server-side storage

When you submit a form, we store the submission in our database to reply to you. That's not a cookie either, but it's worth knowing about. See the [Privacy Policy](/legal/privacy) for what we keep and for how long.

## Opting out

You can clear the `theme` cookie any time from your browser's developer tools or by clearing site data. The site will fall back to your system theme.

Questions about this document? [legal@aliansoftware.net](mailto:legal@aliansoftware.net) — we'll route to the right human.

Build a spec · 5 steps

# Walk yourself through scoping a project.

Describe your problem, pick the industry and pillar, choose a preferred engagement model. We'll generate a structured spec you can copy, email us, or bring to a discovery call.

1. 1Problem
2. 2Industry
3. 3Service
4. 4Model
5. 5Spec

Step 1 · Describe the problem

## In one paragraph, what are you trying to solve?

Specifics beat polish. Mention volume, current handle time, who owns it, and what's been tried.

0 / 20 minimum characters

Customers

# Teams we've shipped production AI for.

Real engagements across manufacturing, SaaS, e-commerce, healthcare, and the creator economy. Some are public — most anonymized at the client's request.

- 20+

Production engagements
- 5

Published case studies
- 84%

Retainer renewal rate
- 100%

Code transferred on day one

## Engaged with us in the last 18 months

- Alian Software

Parent · Enterprise software
- Manufacturing client

Sheet metal · 40 CNC machines
- SaaS DevTools

B2B SaaS · 1,400+ tickets / mo
- Workflow SaaS

B2B SaaS · 800 inbound leads / mo
- Creator channel

240K subs · finance education
- D2C apparel

Mid-market · multi-region
- Mid-market ERP

Manufacturing · multi-plant
- Pre-Series B startup

Healthcare ops · HIPAA-bound

Real logos land here as client sign-off comes through. [See the 5 published case studies →](/work)

## Want to be on this page?

We don't add logos without sign-off. If you'd rather stay anonymous in the engagement, that's the default — we'll only ask about logo use after shipping.

Their ops team had years of muscle memory with it. Latency requirements were 'sub-second', which Postgres handles trivially. Kafka would have added a 4-week setup tax for zero functional gain.\"}]]}]\n6:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"rounded-xl border-l-4 border-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)] bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] p-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":\"What we learned\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]\",\"children\":\"When the obvious answer adds operational complexity without buying performance you need, the boring answer is correct. We default to Postgres for event buses now and only reach for Kafka when message volume genuinely demands it.\"}]]}]\n"])self.\_\_next\_f.push([1,"7:[\"$\",\"article\",\"Vendor shadcn/ui primitives rather than install\",{\"className\":\"rounded-2xl border border-[color:var(--color-border-token)] bg-[color:var(--background)] p-6 sm:p-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"font-display-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":[\"Decision #\",2]}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-xl font-semibold text-[color:var(--color-text)] sm:text-2xl\",\"children\":\"Vendor shadcn/ui primitives rather than install\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-5 space-y-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Context\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"shadcn/ui is the standard React component library for modern Next.js sites. The standard pattern is `npx shadcn add button`. We hand-copied the source into our repo.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Why we made this call\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"We didn't want surprise updates to break component variants when a maintainer changes class names. Vendoring is 50 lines per primitive — trivial to maintain. We get full customization control and zero version-pinning anxiety.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"rounded-xl border-l-4 border-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)] bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] p-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":\"What we learned\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]\",\"children\":\"For surface-area components you'll customize heavily, vendoring beats installing. For deeply integrated frameworks (auth, routing), install. The line is 'do I want to customize the API itself?'\"}]]}]]}]]}]\n"])self.\_\_next\_f.push([1,"8:[\"$\",\"article\",\"Server-Sent Events over WebSockets for streaming\",{\"className\":\"rounded-2xl border border-[color:var(--color-border-token)] bg-[color:var(--background)] p-6 sm:p-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"font-display-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":[\"Decision #\",3]}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-xl font-semibold text-[color:var(--color-text)] sm:text-2xl\",\"children\":\"Server-Sent Events over WebSockets for streaming\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-5 space-y-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Context\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"Streaming AI responses in 2026 — Vercel AI SDK uses SSE. Most production AI infra uses WebSockets for 'real-time.' We default to SSE.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Why we made this call\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"SSE is half the complexity. One-way streaming is what AI generation actually needs. Reconnection is built into the protocol. Cleaner mental model for the team and for code reviewers from outside our team.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"rounded-xl border-l-4 border-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)] bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] p-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":\"What we learned\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]\",\"children\":\"Use the simpler protocol when the simpler protocol is enough. WebSockets pay off when you need bidirectional, low-latency. AI streaming isn't that.\"}]]}]]}]]}]\n"])self.\_\_next\_f.push([1,"9:[\"$\",\"article\",\"Refusal patterns at the orchestration layer, not in the system prompt\",{\"className\":\"rounded-2xl border border-[color:var(--color-border-token)] bg-[color:var(--background)] p-6 sm:p-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"font-display-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":[\"Decision #\",4]}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-xl font-semibold text-[color:var(--color-text)] sm:text-2xl\",\"children\":\"Refusal patterns at the orchestration layer, not in the system prompt\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-5 space-y-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Context\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"The common pattern is to put refusal instructions in the system prompt: 'refuse if X, refuse if Y...' We moved most of those to deterministic checks in code, before the model is even called.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Why we made this call\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"Prompt-based refusal is probabilistic. Code-based refusal is deterministic. For categories like 'never quote pricing,' you don't want a 95% refusal rate — you want 100%. The model decides intent; the code decides allow/deny.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"rounded-xl border-l-4 border-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)] bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] p-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":\"What we learned\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]\",\"children\":\"Use the model where it shines (intent classification, nuanced understanding). Don't use it where determinism matters more (categorical refusals, action allowlists).\"}]]}]]}]]}]\n"])self.\_\_next\_f.push([1,"a:[\"$\",\"article\",\"We turn down 1 in 8 engagements we get to discovery on\",{\"className\":\"rounded-2xl border border-[color:var(--color-border-token)] bg-[color:var(--background)] p-6 sm:p-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"font-display-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":[\"Decision #\",5]}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-xl font-semibold text-[color:var(--color-text)] sm:text-2xl\",\"children\":\"We turn down 1 in 8 engagements we get to discovery on\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-5 space-y-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Context\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"Most agencies say yes to every engagement that pays. We say no when the ROI math doesn't support the build, when the data isn't there, or when the named owner doesn't exist.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-muted)]\",\"children\":\"Why we made this call\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]/90\",\"children\":\"Fixed-fee economics break if you take on bad-fit work. One unhappy client costs more than five neutral ones in reputation alone. Saying no protects the fixed-fee model's integrity.\"}]]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"rounded-xl border-l-4 border-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)] bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] p-4\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-[color:var(--color-brand-accent)]\",\"children\":\"What we learned\"}],[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"className\":\"mt-2 text-[15px] leading-relaxed text-[color:var(--color-text)]\",\"children\":\"Saying no in discovery is the kindest thing you can do for a client who'd otherwise burn $80K on a build that wouldn't ship. [EN](/en/decisions)[HI](/hi/decisions)

Non-obvious decisions

# Forks in the road we took the less-obvious way.

Ten decisions where the obvious answer would have been a mistake. Tech choices, engagement structure, hiring cadence. The context, the why, and what we learned.

Decision #1

## Postgres over Kafka for the multi-agent event bus

Context

When we built the Multi-Agent Manufacturing System, the obvious answer for inter-agent communication was Kafka. We picked Postgres.

Why we made this call

The client already ran Postgres. Their ops team had years of muscle memory with it. Latency requirements were 'sub-second', which Postgres handles trivially. Kafka would have added a 4-week setup tax for zero functional gain.

What we learned

When the obvious answer adds operational complexity without buying performance you need, the boring answer is correct. We default to Postgres for event buses now and only reach for Kafka when message volume genuinely demands it.

Decision #2

## Vendor shadcn/ui primitives rather than install

Context

shadcn/ui is the standard React component library for modern Next.js sites. The standard pattern is `npx shadcn add button`. We hand-copied the source into our repo.

Why we made this call

We didn't want surprise updates to break component variants when a maintainer changes class names. Vendoring is 50 lines per primitive — trivial to maintain. We get full customization control and zero version-pinning anxiety.

What we learned

For surface-area components you'll customize heavily, vendoring beats installing. For deeply integrated frameworks (auth, routing), install. The line is 'do I want to customize the API itself?'

Decision #3

## Server-Sent Events over WebSockets for streaming

Context

Streaming AI responses in 2026 — Vercel AI SDK uses SSE. Most production AI infra uses WebSockets for 'real-time.' We default to SSE.

Why we made this call

SSE is half the complexity. One-way streaming is what AI generation actually needs. Reconnection is built into the protocol. Cleaner mental model for the team and for code reviewers from outside our team.

What we learned

Use the simpler protocol when the simpler protocol is enough. WebSockets pay off when you need bidirectional, low-latency. AI streaming isn't that.

Decision #4

## Refusal patterns at the orchestration layer, not in the system prompt

Context

The common pattern is to put refusal instructions in the system prompt: 'refuse if X, refuse if Y...' We moved most of those to deterministic checks in code, before the model is even called.

Why we made this call

Prompt-based refusal is probabilistic. Code-based refusal is deterministic. For categories like 'never quote pricing,' you don't want a 95% refusal rate — you want 100%. The model decides intent; the code decides allow/deny.

What we learned

Use the model where it shines (intent classification, nuanced understanding). Don't use it where determinism matters more (categorical refusals, action allowlists).

Decision #5

## We turn down 1 in 8 engagements we get to discovery on

Context

Most agencies say yes to every engagement that pays. We say no when the ROI math doesn't support the build, when the data isn't there, or when the named owner doesn't exist.

Why we made this call

Fixed-fee economics break if you take on bad-fit work. One unhappy client costs more than five neutral ones in reputation alone. Saying no protects the fixed-fee model's integrity.

What we learned

Saying no in discovery is the kindest thing you can do for a client who'd otherwise burn $80K on a build that wouldn't ship. The retainer renewal economics make up for the lost top-line.

Decision #6

## Eval suites in client repos, not ours

Context

Most agencies keep their eval suites internal. We put eval suites in the client's repo from day one.

Why we made this call

When we leave, we want the eval suite to keep running. If it lives in our infra, it dies with the engagement. In their repo, with their CI, owned by their named human — it's sticky.

What we learned

If you want a deliverable to outlive your engagement, put it where the client lives, not where you live. Even at the cost of more setup work upfront.

Decision #7

## $25/hour as the public starting rate

Context

Standard practice for offshore AI agencies is to obscure rates and quote per-engagement. We publish the $25/hour rate on the site.

Why we made this call

It filters the funnel ruthlessly. Anyone shocked by $25/hour isn't our buyer. Anyone who's been quoted $150-250/hour by a US agency sees us as exceptional value. Public pricing collapses bad-fit conversations early.

What we learned

Public, specific pricing is a sorting mechanism. It costs a tiny number of high-budget deals we wouldn't have won anyway, and saves dozens of hours of mis-qualified discovery calls.

Decision #8

## We hire when demand calls, not on a growth curve

Context

Standard agency playbook: hire ahead of pipeline. We hire when client demand commits us to it.

Why we made this call

Hiring ahead of demand requires you to win every pitch to feed the bench. That pressure leads to taking bad-fit work. Hiring against signed commitments removes that pressure entirely.

What we learned

Your hiring cadence is a strategic lever, not just a cost lever. Slower hiring means you can say no more. Saying no more means better client outcomes. Better outcomes mean better referrals. Compounds.

Decision #9

## We don't claim AI quality SLAs — we claim audit-trail SLAs

Context

Clients sometimes ask for 'X% accuracy SLA' on AI features. We won't sign that. We will sign 'every output has a replayable audit trail within Y minutes' and 'every regression caught in production becomes a permanent eval case within Z business days.'

Why we made this call

Quality SLAs on probabilistic systems are dishonest. Audit-trail and response-time SLAs on the system around the AI are honest and measurable. Auditors actually prefer the latter.

What we learned

Build SLAs around the deterministic parts of your AI system, not the probabilistic ones. Auditors agree. Clients adapt to it quickly once you explain why.

Decision #10

## TypeScript on the frontend, Python on the backend, no GraphQL

Context

Common pattern: monorepo with GraphQL throughout. We separate cleanly — TS on the browser, Python where AI/ML happens, REST in between.

Why we made this call

GraphQL adds complexity that pays off for graph-shaped data with N+1 problems. Our workloads don't have that. REST + structured JSON keeps the team able to read each other's PRs without specialization.

What we learned

Pick technologies because they fit your shape of work, not because they're trending. Boring is reliable. Reliable wins compound. Compound interest funds the next clever choice when it actually matters.

## Want to talk through a decision you're facing?

Book a 20-min call. We'll share our take and the trade-off we'd be watching for. No commitment beyond.

[Book a 20-min call →](/book-a-demo)[Read our principles](/principles)

Risks + mitigations

# What can go wrong — and how we mitigate.

The ten risks we actually plan for on every engagement. Honest severity ratings, the mitigations we build in by default, and what's still on you to own.

## Hallucination — confidently wrong

High

The model generates plausible-sounding but false content. Especially dangerous in customer-facing or regulated contexts.

How we mitigate

- Citation-required prompting on every fact-bearing response
- Retrieval-augmented generation with hybrid search + reranking
- Refusal patterns when retrieval confidence is below threshold
- Eval suite that measures hallucination rate weekly
- Human review gate on high-stakes outputs (medical, legal, financial)

## Drift — silent quality decay over time

High

Model behavior shifts as upstream model providers release updates, your data evolves, or edge cases accumulate. Quality dies quietly.

How we mitigate

- Weekly eval-suite replay against the full case library
- Drift alerting on score deltas above a threshold
- Production failures become permanent eval cases
- Named human owner of the eval suite — non-optional in our SOW
- Quarterly drift retrospective with the client

## Prompt injection — user manipulating the agent

Medium-High

Users craft inputs that make the agent ignore system instructions, leak prompts, or take unintended actions.

How we mitigate

- Input sanitization at the orchestration layer
- Refusal patterns for obvious injection attempts
- Tool-use gating: no agent action without explicit approval workflow
- Logging + alerting on suspected injection attempts
- System prompts that don't contain anything secret you couldn't put on a billboard

## Cost runaway — token bills surprising leadership

Medium

Production AI cost compounds quickly. A change that improves quality 10% but triples cost can blow your budget without notice.

How we mitigate

- Per-conversation cost telemetry from day one
- Prompt caching for static context (system prompts, schemas)
- Tier-down to smaller models for low-stakes turns
- Cost budgets with daily / weekly alerts
- Eval suite logs cost per case — surfaces cost-quality regressions

## PII / PHI exposure

High

Sensitive personal data ends up in prompts, logs, or model training. Compliance, contractual, and ethical consequences.

How we mitigate

- PII detection + redaction at the orchestration layer
- Encryption in transit and at rest, customer-managed keys when required
- Use of enterprise model tiers that exclude data from training
- DPAs with all sub-processors
- Retention policies sized to your obligations, not our defaults

## Auto-execution of irreversible actions

High

Agent sends an email, posts to the ledger, refunds a customer, or merges a PR without proper review.

How we mitigate

- v1 agents draft; humans approve; system executes — never the reverse
- Allowlist of touchable actions, deny-by-default for everything else
- Explicit confirmation tokens for irreversible operations
- Audit log on every executed action with named approver
- Sandbox environments for any agent that writes code or executes

## Vendor lock-in to a single model provider

Medium

Built around one model's quirks. Provider raises prices, deprecates the model, or has an outage — you have no fallback.

How we mitigate

- Abstract the model behind a thin internal interface
- Run the eval suite against 2+ model providers
- Open-source model fallback for non-critical paths
- Cost-comparison telemetry across providers, refreshed quarterly

## Regulatory + audit exposure

Medium-High

Auditor asks 'show me the AI's reasoning on account 4729 on March 14.' If you can't, you have a problem.

How we mitigate

- Replayable reasoning traces — every prompt, retrieval, tool call, output
- Logs tied to named users, named systems, unique decision IDs
- Retention sized to your audit window (often years)
- Model approval workflow with named approver per release
- Refusal patterns documented in plain English for the audit team

## Vendor disappears mid-engagement

Medium

Your AI vendor (us or anyone else) gets acquired, pivots, or shuts down. Your AI is now an orphan.

How we mitigate

- Code, prompts, configs in your repos from day one — not ours
- Infrastructure in your accounts, not ours
- Runbooks written for your team, not for our convenience
- Eval suite owned by a named human on your side
- Quarterly handoff drill — can your team operate this without us?

## Over-automation — losing the human judgment that matters

Medium

Automating workflows that should have kept a human in the loop. Customers feel it. Quality decays in subtle ways. Your team loses skill.

How we mitigate

- Default to human-in-loop on anything emotional, contractual, or strategic
- Sample 5% of automated outputs for human QA review weekly
- Cross-train the team — don't atrophy the skills you'd want back
- Sunset clause in every automation: would we be OK if this stopped tomorrow?

## Want our risk packet for your security review?

Comprehensive document with our threat model, control mappings, and the specific mitigations we'd build for your engagement. Email security@aliansoftware.net and we'll send within a day.

[Request the risk packet](mailto:security@aliansoftware.net?subject=Risk packet request)[Read the Security overview](/security)

Referrals

# Send us a build, take 10% of the first invoice.

Simple, transparent, no platform. If you know a team that should be shipping AI and they end up signing with us, you get paid.

## How it works

- ### Send the intro

Email referrals@aliansoftware.net with the prospect's company, contact, and the problem they're trying to solve. Or use the form below.
- ### We follow up · 1 business day

We email the prospect within 24 hours of your intro. If they're not a fit, we tell them honestly and route them elsewhere. No ghosting, no spam.
- ### Track via your referral ID

Once registered, you get a referral ID. We track every prospect to that ID — through discovery, scoping, signed SOW, paid invoice.
- ### 10% of net first invoice

Paid within 30 days of the prospect's first paid milestone. USD, EUR, GBP, or INR. We don't claw back if they renew (that's just retention math).

### Eligible referrals

- B2B SaaS companies with 50+ employees thinking about AI
- Manufacturing, e-commerce, finance, healthcare, logistics businesses
- Agencies or consultancies looking for a white-label AI delivery partner
- Investors who'd like their portfolio companies to ship AI

### Not eligible

- Companies we already have a sales conversation with
- Solo consultants without budget authority
- Pure 'AI strategy' inquiries with no build appetite

## Send a referral

Tell us who they are, what they're trying to do, and the best way to reach them. We'll come back within a business day.

Name

What kind of conversation?20-min discovery — scope a possible build60-min technical deep-dive — architecture reviewAgency / partner inquiryOther

Send

We reply within one business day. Usually faster.

Prefer email? [referrals@aliansoftware.net](mailto:referrals@aliansoftware.net?subject=Referral)

For agencies and integrators we have a white-label delivery program with bigger margins and dedicated capacity.

Changelog

# What we've shipped.

Updates to the site. New entries on top. Boring entries are healthy entries — they mean we're shipping incrementally and not re-launching every month.

1. May 13, 2026Shipped

## Industry overflow pages + site search

Added dedicated pages for healthcare, finance, real estate, education, and logistics. ⌘K opens site-wide search across services, industries, case studies, and the blog. Breadcrumbs added to every detail page.
2. May 12, 2026Shipped

## Sprint 3 conversion + SEO pass

Industries hub, /process, /contact, /partners, /checklist landing, and 5 blog posts shipped. Sitemap and robots.txt generated. Per-page OG images live for every detail page.
3. May 11, 2026Shipped

## Sprint 2 expansion

Service detail pages, pricing FAQ accordion, case-study pages with hero stats, About, blog with 3 launch posts. Counter band on the homepage, real chat widget against /api/chat.
4. May 10, 2026Shipped

## Sprint 1 — Live Problem Solver

Homepage hero with typing headline, cycling placeholder, and streaming Claude responses. Activity ticker, speed timeline, service pillars, featured case study, final CTA.
5. May 10, 2026Added

## Dark mode + brand system

Inter + JetBrains Mono. brand-primary #253D98 and brand-accent #FF5A5F. Tailwind v4 CSS-first theme.

Want to see what we're building next? [Book a call →](/book-a-demo)
